Description: RQ100 Requirements shall be managed in a 3rd party requirements management tool such as DOORS.
Reason: TC154 describes decomposing a requirement and related event messages/links but does not mention DOORS or any 3rd-party requirements management tool; no evidence that requirements are managed in DOORS.

Description: RQ100 Requirements shall be managed in a 3rd party requirements management tool such as DOORS.
Reason: TC155 describes refining a requirement and temporary links but contains no mention of DOORS or management in a 3rd-party tool.

Description: RQ100 Requirements shall be managed in a 3rd party requirements management tool such as DOORS.
Reason: TC156 describes modifying a requirement and tentative link status but provides no indication that requirements are managed in DOORS or another 3rd-party RM tool.

Description: RQ100 Requirements shall be managed in a 3rd party requirements management tool such as DOORS.
Reason: TC158 describes merging requirements and link reassignment but does not reference DOORS or external requirements management; no evidence for requirement.

Description: RQ100 Requirements shall be managed in a 3rd party requirements management tool such as DOORS.
Reason: This test explicitly references DOORS and shows the system interacts with DOORS to obtain requirement-related information, which provides direct evidence of use of DOORS. However, it is a scenario-specific use (polling for quantitative info during composite event construction) and does not fully demonstrate that requirements are managed in DOORS for all CRUD operations, nor does it show authoritative management, synchronization, or error handling. Therefore coverage is partial.

Description: RQ101 Non-requirement artifacts shall be managed in 3rd party tools such as Rational Rose or Clear case
Reason: TC141 covers establishing a trace between a UML artifact and a requirement but does not assert that non-requirement artifacts are managed in third-party tools like Rational Rose or ClearCase, nor does it test tool-based management, storage, versioning, or synchronization.

Description: RQ101 Non-requirement artifacts shall be managed in 3rd party tools such as Rational Rose or Clear case
Reason: TC142 describes a non-registered subscriber attempting to establish a trace and that no trace is established. It does not mention management of non-requirement artifacts in third-party tools (Rational Rose or ClearCase) or any integration/management behavior required by RQ101.

Description: RQ101 Non-requirement artifacts shall be managed in 3rd party tools such as Rational Rose or Clear case
Reason: TC154 describes decomposing a requirement and creation of temporary links; it addresses requirement-level operations and messaging, not management of non-requirement artifacts in third-party tools like Rational Rose or ClearCase.

Description: RQ101 Non-requirement artifacts shall be managed in 3rd party tools such as Rational Rose or Clear case
Reason: TC155 addresses refining a requirement and establishing temporary links; it does not verify storage or management of non-requirement artifacts in third-party tools (Rational Rose or ClearCase).

Description: RQ101 Non-requirement artifacts shall be managed in 3rd party tools such as Rational Rose or Clear case
Reason: TC157 covers deleting a requirement and related link status changes; it does not address management of non-requirement artifacts in third-party tools named by RQ101.

Description: RQ101 Non-requirement artifacts shall be managed in 3rd party tools such as Rational Rose or Clear case
Reason: TC162 references polling DOORS (a 3rd-party requirements tool) for related requirements' quantitative info. This concerns requirement artifacts in DOORS, not management of non-requirement artifacts in Rational Rose or ClearCase. TC162 does not assert the management, storage, versioning, or synchronization of non-requirement artifacts in the tools named by RQ101.

Description: RQ102 Only registered subscribers shall be allowed to establish traces.
Reason: This test asserts that a registered subscriber can establish a trace (positive/happy path). It demonstrates allowance but does not cover the negative enforcement for non-registered subscribers on its own.

Description: RQ102 Only registered subscribers shall be allowed to establish traces.
Reason: This test explicitly asserts the negative aspect of the requirement (non-registered subscriber attempts trace and no trace is established). It addresses the 'only registered' restriction but does not by itself demonstrate the positive allowance for registered subscribers.

Description: RQ102 Only registered subscribers shall be allowed to establish traces.
Reason: This test verifies the registration action and that a subscriber becomes registered (a necessary precondition for allowing traces). It does not itself assert trace-establishment behavior, but supports the requirement by producing registered subjects.

Description: RQ102 Only registered subscribers shall be allowed to establish traces.
Reason: This test describes a subscriber failing to register and being removed; it does not assert behavior about establishing traces (no direct assertion that an unregistered subscriber attempted a trace and was blocked).

Description: RQ102 Only registered subscribers shall be allowed to establish traces.
Reason: This test describes registration of the subscriber manager with an event server and does not address subscriber trace-establishment permissions.

Description: RQ102 Only registered subscribers shall be allowed to establish traces.
Reason: This test concerns event notification handling for registered subscribers and storage in an event log; it does not address trace-establishment permission.

Description: RQ103 A user shall register as a subscriber.
Reason: TC142 describes behaviour of a non-registered subscriber attempting to establish a trace and the expected denial. This exercises the system's behaviour when not registered but does not verify that users can complete registration.

Description: RQ103 A user shall register as a subscriber.
Reason: TC143 explicitly describes the subscriber performing registration and the postcondition asserting the subscriber is registered. This directly satisfies the requirement's core obligation that a user shall register as a subscriber (happy path and observable postcondition are present).

Description: RQ103 A user shall register as a subscriber.
Reason: TC144 describes a failure / exit from registration and resulting removal from the system. It does not assert or verify that a user can successfully register, so it does not provide positive verification of the requirement.

Description: RQ103 A user shall register as a subscriber.
Reason: TC146 concerns the subscriber manager registering with an event server (manager-level registration), not a user registering as a subscriber; it does not verify the user's registration requirement.

Description: RQ103 A user shall register as a subscriber.
Reason: TC147 addresses real-time event publication and subscriber manager subscriptions; it does not assert a user registration action or its success.

Description: RQ103 A user shall register as a subscriber.
Reason: TC149 concerns event notifications being received and logged by the subscriber manager for already-registered subscribers. It assumes registration but does not verify the registration action itself.

Description: RQ104 On registration a subscriber shall register itself under the control of a subscriber manager
Reason: This test explicitly exercises the registration action and the postcondition that the subscriber is registered under the subscriber manager, directly matching the requirement's statement.

Description: RQ104 On registration a subscriber shall register itself under the control of a subscriber manager
Reason: This test describes the negative path where the subscriber does not register with a subscriber manager and is removed. It verifies an error outcome related to registration behavior but does not assert the successful registration behavior required by RQ104, therefore it provides partial but not full positive coverage.

Description: RQ104 On registration a subscriber shall register itself under the control of a subscriber manager
Reason: This test concerns subscriber manager registration with an event server, not subscriber registration under a subscriber manager; no direct evidence for RQ104 is present.

Description: RQ104 On registration a subscriber shall register itself under the control of a subscriber manager
Reason: This test describes event publication to subscriber managers and does not include assertions about subscriber registration under a subscriber manager.

Description: RQ104 On registration a subscriber shall register itself under the control of a subscriber manager
Reason: This test exercises event notification storage by a subscriber manager and assumes registered subscribers, but does not verify the registration action required by RQ104.

Description: RQ104 On registration a subscriber shall register itself under the control of a subscriber manager
Reason: This test covers event notification processing by a subscriber manager given registered subscribers; it does not assert subscriber registration behavior itself.

Description: RQ105 The user shall establish traces between requirements and other types of artifacts by establishing a subscription from an artifact to the relevant requirement
Reason: The test explicitly instructs a subscriber to establish a trace between a UML artifact and a requirement and asserts as a postcondition that the trace is established. This directly implements the requirement's action (establishing traces) and mechanism (subscription/trace from an artifact to a requirement).

Description: RQ105 The user shall establish traces between requirements and other types of artifacts by establishing a subscription from an artifact to the relevant requirement
Reason: This test exercises a negative/error scenario (unregistered subscriber attempting to establish a trace) which is directly related to the subscription-based trace establishment mechanism in the requirement. It verifies an error path (subscription not allowed for unregistered subscriber) but does not demonstrate successful subscription/trace establishment itself.

Description: RQ105 The user shall establish traces between requirements and other types of artifacts by establishing a subscription from an artifact to the relevant requirement
Reason: This test asserts that a subscriber manager has already subscribed to requirements and that events are published; it does not exercise or assert the act of establishing a subscription/trace from an artifact to a requirement.

Description: RQ105 The user shall establish traces between requirements and other types of artifacts by establishing a subscription from an artifact to the relevant requirement
Reason: This test concerns refining requirements and creating temporary links between requirements themselves; it does not demonstrate establishing a subscription from an artifact to a requirement.

Description: RQ105 The user shall establish traces between requirements and other types of artifacts by establishing a subscription from an artifact to the relevant requirement
Reason: The test focuses on event server behavior (constructing composite event messages, identifying related requirements) rather than on a user or artifact establishing a subscription/trace from an artifact to a requirement. It does not assert creation of subscriptions/traces.

Description: RQ106 Each artifact shall be placed under the control of a subscriber manager
Reason: The test demonstrates a subscriber being registered under a subscriber manager (an example of an artifact being placed under subscriber-manager control). It exercises the happy-path registration for one subscriber but does not assert that every artifact (all types, or all instances) is placed under such control, nor does it show enforcement for all artifact types or lifecycle events.

Description: RQ106 Each artifact shall be placed under the control of a subscriber manager
Reason: This test covers a negative/error path related to registration: it shows the system removing a subscriber that does not register under a subscriber manager. This is relevant to enforcement of the requirement but does not show that every artifact is placed under control (it shows removal when registration is not completed), nor does it cover other artifact types, or guarantee that artifacts are assigned to a subscriber manager instead of being removed.

Description: RQ106 Each artifact shall be placed under the control of a subscriber manager
Reason: This test verifies subscriber manager registration with the event server. It does not demonstrate that artifacts are placed under the control of a subscriber manager.

Description: RQ106 Each artifact shall be placed under the control of a subscriber manager
Reason: This test indicates that subscriber managers subscribe to requirements and receive notifications for changed requirements; this implies an association between requirements (artifacts) and subscriber managers but does not explicitly assert that each artifact is placed under a subscriber manager's control or how that control is ensured for all artifacts.

Description: RQ106 Each artifact shall be placed under the control of a subscriber manager
Reason: This test shows the event server linking requirements to the same artifact and sending customized messages to subscribers. It demonstrates artifact-to-subscriber relationships in event distribution, which is relevant, but does not prove that every artifact is managed/placed under a subscriber manager or how assignment/ownership is established and maintained.

Description: RQ106 Each artifact shall be placed under the control of a subscriber manager
Reason: This test concerns event processing order and prevention of out-of-order processing. It does not address placement of artifacts under subscriber manager control.

Description: RQ107 The user shall be able to delete subscriptions.
Reason: TC143 covers subscriber registration with a manager. This is about creating/adding subscriptions or registration, not deleting subscriptions.

Description: RQ107 The user shall be able to delete subscriptions.
Reason: TC144 covers a subscriber failing to register and being removed from the system; it does not describe deleting subscriptions and does not assert subscription deletion.

Description: RQ107 The user shall be able to delete subscriptions.
Reason: TC145 explicitly exercises deletion of subscriptions: the steps state the user deletes the subscription and the postcondition asserts the subscription has been deleted. This directly satisfies the requirement's capability.

Description: RQ107 The user shall be able to delete subscriptions.
Reason: TC147 describes event publication to subscriber managers. It does not exercise or assert deletion of subscriptions.

Description: RQ107 The user shall be able to delete subscriptions.
Reason: TC157 concerns deleting a requirement (setting requirement obsolete and link status changes). This is a different domain object (requirement vs subscription) and does not assert subscription deletion.

Description: RQ108 A subscriber manager shall register itself with the eventserver so that it may receive relevant event messages.
Reason: This test describes a subscriber registering with a subscriber manager, which is a different registration flow (subscriber -> subscriber manager) and does not address the subscriber manager registering with the eventserver or receiving eventserver-sent messages.

Description: RQ108 A subscriber manager shall register itself with the eventserver so that it may receive relevant event messages.
Reason: This test explicitly covers the registration action (subscriber manager registers IP/port/name with the event server), directly addressing the 'register itself with the eventserver' phrase. It does not by itself assert that messages are subsequently received, so it partially covers the full requirement.

Description: RQ108 A subscriber manager shall register itself with the eventserver so that it may receive relevant event messages.
Reason: This test shows that a subscriber manager can retrieve missed notifications from the event server when coming online (evidence of receiving messages). It does not assert that the subscriber manager registered itself with the eventserver, so it is a partial match.

Description: RQ108 A subscriber manager shall register itself with the eventserver so that it may receive relevant event messages.
Reason: This test explicitly verifies that a subscriber manager receives an event notification (the 'receive relevant event messages' portion). It does not assert that the subscriber manager has registered itself with the eventserver, so it only partially covers the requirement.

Description: RQ108 A subscriber manager shall register itself with the eventserver so that it may receive relevant event messages.
Reason: This test verifies that a subscriber manager receives and processes event notifications (covers the 'receive relevant event messages' portion). It does not state that the subscriber manager registered itself with the eventserver, so it only partially covers the requirement.

Description: RQ108 A subscriber manager shall register itself with the eventserver so that it may receive relevant event messages.
Reason: This test documents the event server locating subscriber manager addresses and sending notifications that are received by subscriber managers (covers the 'receive relevant event messages' portion and implies the server knows subscriber manager addresses). It does not explicitly state the registration action by the subscriber manager, so it is a partial match; combined with a registration test it helps fully satisfy the requirement.

Description: RQ109 If the subscriber manager is online; the push model shall be used to send it event notifications in real time.
Reason: The test explicitly sets the subscriber manager online and describes an event being sent in real time to the relevant subscriber manager. This semantically matches the requirement's core behavior (online -> server sends notification). The test does not explicitly assert or distinguish the delivery mechanism as a 'push model' vs. other transport semantics, nor does it include measurable timing bounds or error conditions; therefore confidence is partial.

Description: RQ109 If the subscriber manager is online; the push model shall be used to send it event notifications in real time.
Reason: This test describes a subscriber manager that was offline and then downloads missed events when it comes online (pull-style retrieval). It does not verify that when the subscriber manager is online the server uses a push model in real time.

Description: RQ109 If the subscriber manager is online; the push model shall be used to send it event notifications in real time.
Reason: The test documents that an event notification is sent to and received by the subscriber manager and stored locally, which aligns with the requirement's intent (server-initiated delivery). It lacks an explicit statement that the delivery uses a 'push model' or timing/latency assertions; hence partial confidence.

Description: RQ109 If the subscriber manager is online; the push model shall be used to send it event notifications in real time.
Reason: This test shows notifications being sent and processed by the subscriber manager, which supports the requirement's goal of server-initiated delivery. It does not explicitly label the mechanism as 'push' or provide timing guarantees or online-state validation beyond implication; therefore confidence is partial.

Description: RQ109 If the subscriber manager is online; the push model shall be used to send it event notifications in real time.
Reason: This test most directly describes server-side identification of subscriber manager endpoints and actively sending notifications to those managers (server-initiated delivery). It therefore strongly aligns with the push-model requirement. However, it does not explicitly state the manager must be online prior to sending, nor specifies timing constraints that would prove 'real time' beyond the immediate send action; therefore confidence is partial.

Description: RQ110 Whenever the subscriber manager first comes on line; it shall implement the pull model to retrieve outstanding event messages from the eventserver.
Reason: This test verifies registration with the event server; it does not verify retrieval of outstanding messages when coming online.

Description: RQ110 Whenever the subscriber manager first comes on line; it shall implement the pull model to retrieve outstanding event messages from the eventserver.
Reason: This test describes real-time publication and notification delivery while a subscriber manager is online, not the startup pull retrieval behavior.

Description: RQ110 Whenever the subscriber manager first comes on line; it shall implement the pull model to retrieve outstanding event messages from the eventserver.
Reason: The test explicitly sets the manager offline, brings it online, and states as a postcondition that the manager has successfully downloaded outstanding notifications from the event server. This verifies the observable outcome required by the requirement (outstanding messages are retrieved when coming online). However the test does not explicitly assert that the retrieval was performed via the 'pull model' (i.e., initiated by the subscriber manager rather than pushed by the server), nor does it exercise boundary/error cases (multiple outstanding messages, retries, authentication/timeouts). Therefore it semantically aligns with the requirement's core behavior but lacks explicit verification of the specified mechanism and edge conditions.

Description: RQ110 Whenever the subscriber manager first comes on line; it shall implement the pull model to retrieve outstanding event messages from the eventserver.
Reason: This test describes reception and storage of a non-speculative event notification during normal operation, not retrieval on initial startup via pull model.

Description: RQ110 Whenever the subscriber manager first comes on line; it shall implement the pull model to retrieve outstanding event messages from the eventserver.
Reason: This test describes receiving a speculative event notification while manager is registered; it does not exercise startup retrieval of outstanding messages or the pull model behavior.

Description: RQ110 Whenever the subscriber manager first comes on line; it shall implement the pull model to retrieve outstanding event messages from the eventserver.
Reason: This test concerns processing order of events already received by the subscriber manager; it does not address retrieval on first coming online or the pull model.

Description: RQ111 A subscriber manager shall receive event notifications on behalf of the artifacts that it manages.
Reason: This test describes an event being published and that an event notification is sent to the relevant subscriber manager but does not assert that the subscriber manager actually receives, processes, or stores the notification (no postcondition). It documents sending only, so it does not provide direct verification of receipt.

Description: RQ111 A subscriber manager shall receive event notifications on behalf of the artifacts that it manages.
Reason: This test checks the subscriber manager retrieving (downloading) previously undelivered event notifications when it comes online, which is a concrete receipt behaviour fulfilling the requirement.

Description: RQ111 A subscriber manager shall receive event notifications on behalf of the artifacts that it manages.
Reason: This test explicitly states that the relevant subscriber manager receives the event notification for a registered subscriber and stores the message locally, directly satisfying the requirement's core behaviour (receipt on behalf of managed artifacts).

Description: RQ111 A subscriber manager shall receive event notifications on behalf of the artifacts that it manages.
Reason: This test explicitly asserts receipt by the relevant subscriber manager and further specifies processing of the notification, demonstrating the manager acting on behalf of subscribers.

Description: RQ111 A subscriber manager shall receive event notifications on behalf of the artifacts that it manages.
Reason: This test documents the event server sending notifications and explicitly states the event message is received by each relevant subscriber manager 'on behalf of its managed subscribers', directly matching the requirement wording.

Description: RQ111 A subscriber manager shall receive event notifications on behalf of the artifacts that it manages.
Reason: The test demonstrates that event messages have a received order, which is topically related to receipt, but it exercises/display-level ordering and does not explicitly assert that the subscriber manager received notifications on behalf of artifacts. Therefore it is only weakly relevant.

Description: RQ112 A subscriber manager shall manage traces for one or more artifacts.
Reason: This test demonstrates the core happy path of creating a trace between an artifact and a requirement, which is a central aspect of 'managing traces'. However, it only shows a single trace creation scenario and provides no evidence for management across multiple artifacts, lifecycle operations (update/delete), querying, or persistence.

Description: RQ112 A subscriber manager shall manage traces for one or more artifacts.
Reason: This test verifies a negative management behavior (preventing trace creation by a non-registered subscriber). That demonstrates part of 'managing traces' (access/control and prevention) but does not demonstrate general management capabilities (multi-artifact handling, lifecycle operations, querying, persistence).

Description: RQ112 A subscriber manager shall manage traces for one or more artifacts.
Reason: This test verifies subscriber registration with the subscriber manager (precondition for trace operations) but does not itself assert any trace management behavior.

Description: RQ112 A subscriber manager shall manage traces for one or more artifacts.
Reason: This test concerns registering the subscriber manager with an event server; it does not exercise or assert trace management functionality.

Description: RQ112 A subscriber manager shall manage traces for one or more artifacts.
Reason: This test verifies event notification receipt and logging, which is related to event handling but does not provide evidence that the subscriber manager manages traces for artifacts.

Description: RQ113 On receipt of an event message; the subscriber manager shall differentiate between speculative and evolutionary events.
Reason: TC149 documents receipt and storage of a non-speculative event notification. It demonstrates handling for non-speculative events but does not include an explicit assertion that the subscriber manager distinguishes between speculative and evolutionary events upon receipt.

Description: RQ113 On receipt of an event message; the subscriber manager shall differentiate between speculative and evolutionary events.
Reason: TC150 documents receipt of a speculative event notification by the subscriber manager (happy-path receipt). It shows handling of speculative notifications but does not explicitly state the subscriber manager parses and differentiates event types or that it distinguishes speculative vs evolutionary on receipt.

Description: RQ113 On receipt of an event message; the subscriber manager shall differentiate between speculative and evolutionary events.
Reason: TC152 explicitly asserts that the subscriber manager parses an incoming event to determine that it is speculative and then exercises processing paths for speculative events. This directly demonstrates classification for speculative events but does not demonstrate recognition/classification of evolutionary events or show a comparative differentiation behavior between the two types.

Description: RQ113 On receipt of an event message; the subscriber manager shall differentiate between speculative and evolutionary events.
Reason: TC161 describes the event server sending an evolutionary event to subscriber managers and that the message is received, but it does not assert that the subscriber manager classifies the message as evolutionary or otherwise differentiates message types upon receipt.

Description: RQ113 On receipt of an event message; the subscriber manager shall differentiate between speculative and evolutionary events.
Reason: TC162 is focused on the event server constructing composite speculative event messages and delivering them to subscribers; it does not exercise or assert subscriber manager behavior for differentiating event types upon receipt.

Description: RQ114 The subscriber manager shall store information for evolutionary events in an eventlog.
Reason: TC146 concerns registration of the subscriber manager with the event server and does not address storing evolutionary event information.

Description: RQ114 The subscriber manager shall store information for evolutionary events in an eventlog.
Reason: TC148 states the subscriber manager downloads missed event notifications when coming online, but does not explicitly assert that it stores those events in a local eventlog. Ambiguous; no explicit storage assertion.

Description: RQ114 The subscriber manager shall store information for evolutionary events in an eventlog.
Reason: TC149 explicitly states that upon receiving a non-speculative event notification the subscriber manager stores the event notification message in the local event log. Given the test suite's terminology (events categorized as speculative or evolutionary), non-speculative maps to evolutionary; the test directly verifies the required storage behavior.

Description: RQ114 The subscriber manager shall store information for evolutionary events in an eventlog.
Reason: TC150 describes processing of speculative event notifications but does not assert storage of evolutionary events in an eventlog.

Description: RQ114 The subscriber manager shall store information for evolutionary events in an eventlog.
Reason: TC152 describes processing of speculative events and model execution/restore; it does not assert that evolutionary (non-speculative) events are stored in an eventlog. No storage evidence.

Description: RQ114 The subscriber manager shall store information for evolutionary events in an eventlog.
Reason: TC159 shows the event server stores events and categorizes them as speculative or evolutionary, but this verifies the event server's logging, not the subscriber manager's storage requirement.

Description: RQ114 The subscriber manager shall store information for evolutionary events in an eventlog.
Reason: TC161 references an 'evolutionary event' and delivery to subscriber managers but contains no assertion that the subscriber manager stores the event in an eventlog. Topically related (event delivered) but does not verify the storage requirement.

Description: RQ115 The user shall be able to construct speculative queries using a GUI.
Reason: TC150 deals with receipt and processing of a speculative event notification by the subscriber manager (title and steps mention speculative notification). It exercises the post-issuance path but does not verify GUI-based construction of the speculative query itself.

Description: RQ115 The user shall be able to construct speculative queries using a GUI.
Reason: TC151 explicitly instructs the user to construct a speculative query using a GUI tool and records the resulting event; this directly verifies the requirement's capability statement (happy path).

Description: RQ115 The user shall be able to construct speculative queries using a GUI.
Reason: TC152 verifies downstream processing of a speculative query after issuance (parsing, injection, execution, restoration). It does not assert how the speculative query was constructed nor that a GUI was used, so it only partially relates to the requirement.

Description: RQ115 The user shall be able to construct speculative queries using a GUI.
Reason: TC155 concerns refining a requirement via a refine event and linkage establishment; it does not reference speculative queries or GUI construction, so it does not verify the requirement.

Description: RQ115 The user shall be able to construct speculative queries using a GUI.
Reason: TC156 covers modifying a requirement and related message/link status changes; it is unrelated to constructing speculative queries through a GUI and provides no verification evidence.

Description: RQ115 The user shall be able to construct speculative queries using a GUI.
Reason: TC162 addresses event-server behavior after receiving a speculative event message (composing and sending customized messages). It demonstrates handling of speculative events but contains no assertion that a GUI was used to construct the speculative query.

Description: RQ116 The subscriber manager shall immediately process speculative events.
Reason: This test concerns non-speculative event notifications and storage in a local event log; it does not address speculative events or their immediate processing.

Description: RQ116 The subscriber manager shall immediately process speculative events.
Reason: This test verifies that the subscriber manager receives speculative event notifications and that they are 'processed according to predefined protocols', so it shows the processing path exists. It does not assert immediacy (no timing, latency, or prioritization checks) nor detail the speculative-processing steps, so it only partially verifies the requirement.

Description: RQ116 The subscriber manager shall immediately process speculative events.
Reason: This test explicitly exercises the subscriber manager receiving and processing a speculative event and returning a response; it covers the functional processing steps. However, the requirement's keyword 'immediately' implies timing/latency or priority behavior which the test does not assert (no timing, timeout, or concurrency/backlog/error handling checks). Therefore core behavior covered but timing requirement is not verified.

Description: RQ116 The subscriber manager shall immediately process speculative events.
Reason: This test describes the event server constructing composite messages for speculative events; it targets the event server behavior, not the subscriber manager's immediate processing requirement.

Description: RQ116 The subscriber manager shall immediately process speculative events.
Reason: This test covers user attempts to process events in incorrect order; it does not target speculative events or assert immediate processing by the subscriber manager.

Description: RQ117 The subscriber manager shall re execute the model using the speculative values.
Reason: TC149 describes handling a non-speculative event and storing it; it does not address re-executing the model with speculative values.

Description: RQ117 The subscriber manager shall re execute the model using the speculative values.
Reason: TC150 describes receiving a speculative event notification but does not assert injection of speculative values or re-execution of the model.

Description: RQ117 The subscriber manager shall re execute the model using the speculative values.
Reason: TC151 describes user construction of a speculative query and that a speculative event message is received by the event server; it does not state that the subscriber manager re-executes the model using speculative values.

Description: RQ117 The subscriber manager shall re execute the model using the speculative values.
Reason: TC152 explicitly states that the subscriber manager injects values (speculative values) into the performance model and re-executes it, then restores the model and returns results — directly matching the requirement.

Description: RQ117 The subscriber manager shall re execute the model using the speculative values.
Reason: TC162 describes the event server constructing composite event messages and polling DOORS; it does not state that the subscriber manager injects speculative values or re-executes the model.

Description: RQ118 The subscriber manager shall report results indicating whether the speculative change negatively impacts other requirements and constraints.
Reason: TC147 concerns publishing an event in real time and subscription presence; it does not assert subscriber manager analysis or reporting of negative impacts to other requirements/constraints.

Description: RQ118 The subscriber manager shall report results indicating whether the speculative change negatively impacts other requirements and constraints.
Reason: TC149 describes receiving and storing non-speculative event notifications in a local event log. It does not address speculative processing, execution of a model, or reporting negative impacts on other requirements/constraints.

Description: RQ118 The subscriber manager shall report results indicating whether the speculative change negatively impacts other requirements and constraints.
Reason: TC150 indicates the subscriber manager receives a speculative event and processes it "according to predefined protocols" but provides no assertion that the processing produces or reports whether speculative changes negatively impact other requirements and constraints.

Description: RQ118 The subscriber manager shall report results indicating whether the speculative change negatively impacts other requirements and constraints.
Reason: TC151 describes user construction of a speculative query and that a speculative event is sent to the event server; it does not assert subscriber manager reporting about negative impacts on other requirements/constraints.

Description: RQ118 The subscriber manager shall report results indicating whether the speculative change negatively impacts other requirements and constraints.
Reason: TC152 demonstrates the subscriber manager executes a performance model, collates results, and returns a response for a speculative event — showing it produces and returns results. However, the test does not explicitly assert that the returned results indicate whether the speculative change negatively impacts other requirements and constraints (no explicit check/statement about negative-impact detection or reporting).

Description: RQ118 The subscriber manager shall report results indicating whether the speculative change negatively impacts other requirements and constraints.
Reason: TC162 shows the event server includes related requirements and quantitative constraints in the customized event message sent to subscribers. This is relevant context (supplies cross-requirement constraints) but it is not an explicit assertion that the subscriber manager reports whether the speculative change negatively impacts those requirements/constraints. Also, TC162 targets the event server behavior rather than a subscriber manager determination/reporting.

Description: RQ119 The subscriber manager shall restore the executable model to its prior state.
Reason: TC143 involves subscriber registration with the subscriber manager; there is no mention of executing or restoring a model.

Description: RQ119 The subscriber manager shall restore the executable model to its prior state.
Reason: TC144 concerns a subscriber failing to register and subsequent removal; no steps or postconditions reference model execution or restoration.

Description: RQ119 The subscriber manager shall restore the executable model to its prior state.
Reason: TC146 covers subscriber manager registration with the event server; it does not address model execution or restoration.

Description: RQ119 The subscriber manager shall restore the executable model to its prior state.
Reason: TC148 describes the subscriber manager coming online and downloading missed event notifications; it does not mention executing or restoring any executable/performance model.

Description: RQ119 The subscriber manager shall restore the executable model to its prior state.
Reason: TC149 describes receipt and storage of non-speculative event notifications; it does not describe model execution or restoration.

Description: RQ119 The subscriber manager shall restore the executable model to its prior state.
Reason: The test explicitly states that after injecting values and re-executing the performance model, the subscriber manager `restores the model to its prior state` and the postcondition reiterates that `The performance model is restored to its original state`. This directly asserts the required behavior.

Description: RQ120 The prototype system shall utilize a driver to trigger events.
Reason: The add-new-requirement test preconditions include the trigger driver and verifies that an event message is sent, aligning with the requirement's intent for the happy path but not exercising error or boundary cases or explicitly verifying driver usage at implementation level.

Description: RQ120 The prototype system shall utilize a driver to trigger events.
Reason: The test explicitly requires 'The trigger driver is running' and verifies that a decompose event message is sent, which aligns with using a driver to trigger events on the happy path. It does not assert driver invocation, error handling, or boundary conditions.

Description: RQ120 The prototype system shall utilize a driver to trigger events.
Reason: The test states the trigger driver must be running and checks that a refine event message is sent, providing topical coverage of the requirement's happy path but lacking assertions about driver invocation, failures, or boundaries.

Description: RQ120 The prototype system shall utilize a driver to trigger events.
Reason: The modify test requires the trigger driver and verifies a modify event message is sent, supporting the requirement for event triggering in the core path but not documenting driver invocation details or failure modes.

Description: RQ120 The prototype system shall utilize a driver to trigger events.
Reason: The merge test includes the trigger driver precondition and verifies a merge event message is sent; it verifies the happy path but lacks assertions around driver errors, absence, or performance/boundary behavior.

Description: RQ121 Event types shall include adding new requirements.
Reason: This test explicitly names an Add New Requirement test case, the steps include 'The user adds a new requirement', and the postcondition asserts 'A new event message is sent.' These facts directly demonstrate an add-event type and the expected event behavior, satisfying the requirement's intent.

Description: RQ121 Event types shall include adding new requirements.
Reason: The refine test documents adding an additional requirement as part of a 'refine' event and that a refine event message is sent. This shows a pathway where a new requirement is added, but it is not the explicit 'add' event type required by the requirement (it's a different event type with additional semantics). Therefore it partially supports the requirement but does not fully evidence that the system's event types include a dedicated 'add new requirement' event type and associated coverage of add-specific behaviors.

Description: RQ121 Event types shall include adding new requirements.
Reason: TC156 is a modify-event test: it changes existing requirements and asserts a modify event message is sent. It does not create/add new requirements, so it does not verify the requirement.

Description: RQ121 Event types shall include adding new requirements.
Reason: TC157 is a delete-event test setting requirements to obsolete and sending a delete event. It does not exercise adding new requirements or an add event type, so it does not verify the requirement.

Description: RQ121 Event types shall include adding new requirements.
Reason: TC158 is a merge-event test that merges existing requirements and sends a merge event. It does not assert creation of new requirements via an 'add' event, so it does not verify the requirement.

Description: RQ121 Event types shall include adding new requirements.
Reason: This test (TC162) describes constructing composite event messages for a speculate event and polling related requirements for quantitative info. It does not assert or exercise adding new requirements or an add-event type, so it does not verify the requirement.

Description: RQ122 Event types shall include decomposing one requirement into two or more requirements.
Reason: TC154 explicitly exercises a 'decompose' event: preconditions, the user action decomposing an existing requirement into two or more requirements, and post-conditions that the requirement is decomposed and an event message is sent. This directly satisfies the requirement statement.

Description: RQ122 Event types shall include decomposing one requirement into two or more requirements.
Reason: TC155 describes a 'refine' event (adding at least one related requirement) rather than decomposing a single requirement into two or more requirements; it does not assert the decompose behavior required.

Description: RQ122 Event types shall include decomposing one requirement into two or more requirements.
Reason: TC157 covers deletion (setting a requirement obsolete) and does not exercise or assert decomposition of one requirement into multiple requirements.

Description: RQ122 Event types shall include decomposing one requirement into two or more requirements.
Reason: TC158 describes a merge event (combining two or more requirements) which is the inverse operation of decomposition. It does not contain steps or post-conditions that decompose a single requirement into multiple requirements, so it does not verify this requirement.

Description: RQ122 Event types shall include decomposing one requirement into two or more requirements.
Reason: TC162 concerns constructing composite event messages for a 'speculate' event by aggregating related requirements and quantitative information. It does not assert an explicit decompose event splitting a requirement into two or more requirements.

Description: RQ123 Event types shall include refining one requirement by adding an additional requirement to the existing one.
Reason: This test describes adding a new requirement via a 'new' event but does not specify selecting an existing requirement and refining it by adding a related requirement; therefore it does not verify the refine behavior required.

Description: RQ123 Event types shall include refining one requirement by adding an additional requirement to the existing one.
Reason: This test describes a 'decompose' event (decomposing into two or more requirements), which is a different event type than 'refine' and does not assert adding an additional requirement to an existing one.

Description: RQ123 Event types shall include refining one requirement by adding an additional requirement to the existing one.
Reason: The test explicitly exercises a 'refine' event, selects an existing requirement, adds an additional related requirement, and asserts the post-condition that the requirement is refined by adding at least one related requirement. This directly implements the requirement's core behavior and its post-conditions (including message/link behavior mentioned).

Description: RQ123 Event types shall include refining one requirement by adding an additional requirement to the existing one.
Reason: This test addresses a 'modify' event (changing an existing requirement) rather than refining by adding an additional related requirement; it does not assert the refine behavior.

Description: RQ123 Event types shall include refining one requirement by adding an additional requirement to the existing one.
Reason: This test covers a 'merge' event (merging two or more requirements) which is not the refine-by-adding event required by RQ123.

Description: RQ124 Event types shall include modifying a requirement.
Reason: This test covers an 'add new requirement' (new event), not a 'modify' event; it does not assert modify behavior, so it does not verify the requirement.

Description: RQ124 Event types shall include modifying a requirement.
Reason: This test covers a 'decompose' event, not a 'modify' event; it does not assert modify behavior, so it does not verify the requirement.

Description: RQ124 Event types shall include modifying a requirement.
Reason: This test covers a 'refine' event, not a 'modify' event; it does not assert modify behavior, so it does not verify the requirement.

Description: RQ124 Event types shall include modifying a requirement.
Reason: This test explicitly invokes a 'modify' event and asserts the requirement is changed and a modify event message is sent, directly satisfying the requirement that event types include modifying a requirement.

Description: RQ124 Event types shall include modifying a requirement.
Reason: This test covers a 'delete' event, not a 'modify' event; it does not assert modify behavior, so it does not verify the requirement.

Description: RQ125 Event types shall include deleting a requirement.
Reason: This test describes adding a new requirement (new event) and does not mention deletion or a delete event; no assertion in this test verifies the presence of a delete event type.

Description: RQ125 Event types shall include deleting a requirement.
Reason: This test describes decomposing a requirement (decompose event) and does not mention deletion or a delete event; no assertion in this test verifies the presence of a delete event type.

Description: RQ125 Event types shall include deleting a requirement.
Reason: This test describes refining a requirement (refine event) and does not mention deletion or a delete event; no assertion in this test verifies the presence of a delete event type.

Description: RQ125 Event types shall include deleting a requirement.
Reason: This test describes modifying a requirement (modify event) and does not mention deletion or a delete event; no assertion in this test verifies the presence of a delete event type.

Description: RQ125 Event types shall include deleting a requirement.
Reason: The test explicitly names a 'Delete a requirement' test, specifies the user action 'specifies a delete event' and 'deletes' an existing requirement, and asserts a 'delete event message is sent' in post-conditions. These statements directly exercise and verify that a delete event type exists and behaves as expected, satisfying the requirement.

Description: RQ126 Event types shall include merging requirements.
Reason: This test covers adding a new requirement and sending a new event message; it does not reference or assert merging requirements or a merge event type.

Description: RQ126 Event types shall include merging requirements.
Reason: This test exercises a 'decompose' event (splitting a requirement) rather than a merge event and contains no assertions about merging.

Description: RQ126 Event types shall include merging requirements.
Reason: This test covers a 'refine' event (adding related requirements) and does not assert merge behavior or a merge event type.

Description: RQ126 Event types shall include merging requirements.
Reason: This test covers a 'modify' event for requirements; it does not verify the existence or behavior of a merge event.

Description: RQ126 Event types shall include merging requirements.
Reason: This test explicitly names and exercises a 'merge' event, specifies selecting two or more requirements to merge, and asserts post-conditions that the requirements are merged and a merge event message is sent. Those steps and assertions directly implement and verify that an event type for merging requirements exists and behaves as expected.

Description: RQ126 Event types shall include merging requirements.
Reason: This test covers construction of composite event messages for a 'speculate' event and related subscriber notification; it does not mention or assert any merge/merging requirement event.

Description: RQ127 The eventserver shall receive event messages when a requirement changes.
Reason: This test shows that a requirement change results in an event notification being sent (demonstrates trigger/send behavior) but does not assert that the eventserver receives that event message.

Description: RQ127 The eventserver shall receive event messages when a requirement changes.
Reason: This test documents that adding a new requirement results in a new event message being sent; it covers the message generation on requirement change but does not demonstrate the eventserver's receipt of such messages.

Description: RQ127 The eventserver shall receive event messages when a requirement changes.
Reason: This test documents that when a requirement is modified, a modify event message is sent. That demonstrates the 'when a requirement changes -> event message is issued' half of the requirement but does not verify that the eventserver receives that message.

Description: RQ127 The eventserver shall receive event messages when a requirement changes.
Reason: This test explicitly verifies that the event server receives an event message, parses it, and stores/categorizes it. However, it does not explicitly tie the issued event message to a requirement change within the same test; it assumes an event trigger issues a message. Therefore the test fully covers the 'receive' behavior but only partially covers the explicit 'when a requirement changes' causation by itself.

Description: RQ127 The eventserver shall receive event messages when a requirement changes.
Reason: This test assumes as a precondition that the event server has already received an event message and exercises identification of impacted requirements and subscribers. It does not verify that the eventserver receives event messages when a requirement changes (no step connecting requirement change to reception).

Description: RQ127 The eventserver shall receive event messages when a requirement changes.
Reason: This test focuses on constructing and sending composite messages after receiving a speculative event; it neither demonstrates that a requirement change triggers an event nor that the eventserver receives events caused by a requirement change.

Description: RQ128 On receipt of an event message the eventserver shall record the event in an eventlog.
Reason: TC149 asserts that the subscriber manager stores a received event notification in its local event log. This verifies storage in a subscriber manager's log, not that the eventserver recorded the event in the eventserver's event log; therefore it does not constitute direct coverage of the eventserver requirement.

Description: RQ128 On receipt of an event message the eventserver shall record the event in an eventlog.
Reason: TC159 explicitly states that upon receiving an event message the event server 'stores event details in its event log' and the postcondition repeats 'The event is stored in the log.' This directly matches the requirement's action (record the event in an eventlog).

Description: RQ128 On receipt of an event message the eventserver shall record the event in an eventlog.
Reason: TC160 describes identification of impacted requirements and searching for subscribers after receipt of an event, but contains no assertion that the eventserver records the event in an event log.

Description: RQ128 On receipt of an event message the eventserver shall record the event in an eventlog.
Reason: TC161 describes notifying subscribers for evolutionary events (identifying subscriber managers, building and sending notification messages). It does not assert that the eventserver stores the event in an event log.

Description: RQ128 On receipt of an event message the eventserver shall record the event in an eventlog.
Reason: TC162 focuses on constructing and sending composite messages for speculative events and does not assert that the eventserver records the received event in an event log.

Description: RQ129 On receipt of an event; the eventserver shall identify all relevant subscribers for the event.
Reason: The test describes an event being published and a notification being sent to a relevant subscriber manager, but it does not include an assertion or verification step that the event server performed the identification of all relevant subscribers on event receipt. The evidence is ambiguous and does not prove identification logic.

Description: RQ129 On receipt of an event; the eventserver shall identify all relevant subscribers for the event.
Reason: This test addresses the subscriber manager receiving a notification and storing it in a local log. It does not exercise or assert that the event server identified all relevant subscribers upon event receipt.

Description: RQ129 On receipt of an event; the eventserver shall identify all relevant subscribers for the event.
Reason: The test explicitly describes receipt of an event, the server identifying impacted requirements, searching for subscribers, and states as a postcondition that all relevant subscribers are identified — directly matching the requirement's expected behavior and asserting the outcome.

Description: RQ129 On receipt of an event; the eventserver shall identify all relevant subscribers for the event.
Reason: The test assumes as a precondition that "The event server has identified a list of subscribers." It verifies notification building/sending, but does not assert or check that the server performed the identification step upon event receipt.

Description: RQ129 On receipt of an event; the eventserver shall identify all relevant subscribers for the event.
Reason: This test focuses on constructing composite messages for each already-identified subscriber (precondition: "The event server has identified a list of subscribers"). It does not contain assertions that the event server identifies relevant subscribers on receipt of the event.

Description: RQ130 The eventserver shall differentiate between evolutionary and speculative events.
Reason: This test only asserts that a speculative event message is sent/received by the event server; it does not assert that the event server differentiates or classifies the event type.

Description: RQ130 The eventserver shall differentiate between evolutionary and speculative events.
Reason: The test shows that the subscriber manager (not the event server) parses and determines that a message is speculative. This is related but does not provide evidence that the event server itself differentiates events.

Description: RQ130 The eventserver shall differentiate between evolutionary and speculative events.
Reason: The test explicitly states that the event server categorizes each event as speculative or evolutionary and asserts the postcondition that the event is categorized accordingly. This directly implements the requirement's core behavior.

Description: RQ130 The eventserver shall differentiate between evolutionary and speculative events.
Reason: This test demonstrates behavior that follows receipt of an evolutionary event (notification behavior). It therefore corroborates existence of evolutionary-event handling but does not explicitly assert that the event server performed the differentiation step that identified the event as evolutionary.

Description: RQ130 The eventserver shall differentiate between evolutionary and speculative events.
Reason: This test documents handling for a speculative event (constructing a composite message) and thus supports the presence of speculative-event processing. It does not, however, explicitly show that the event server performed the classification step that distinguished this message as speculative.

Description: RQ131 When an evolutionary event occurs; the eventserver shall notify subscribers of the event.
Reason: This test describes sending an event notification when a requirement changes (semantically aligned with evolutionary events). It demonstrates the sending action but lacks explicit assertion of reception or explicit labeling of the event as 'evolutionary'; therefore it partially supports the requirement.

Description: RQ131 When an evolutionary event occurs; the eventserver shall notify subscribers of the event.
Reason: This test verifies that a subscriber manager receives and logs a non-speculative event notification (non-speculative likely overlaps with evolutionary). However it validates the subscriber-side reception/logging rather than explicitly proving the eventserver initiated notification for an evolutionary event.

Description: RQ131 When an evolutionary event occurs; the eventserver shall notify subscribers of the event.
Reason: This test verifies detection and categorization of events as evolutionary, which is a necessary precondition for notification, but it does not assert that the eventserver sends notifications to subscribers when an evolutionary event is identified.

Description: RQ131 When an evolutionary event occurs; the eventserver shall notify subscribers of the event.
Reason: This test explicitly names evolutionary events in the title and preconditions, describes constructing and sending a notification to each relevant subscriber manager, and includes postcondition that the message is received — directly exercising all stated behavior in the requirement.

Description: RQ131 When an evolutionary event occurs; the eventserver shall notify subscribers of the event.
Reason: This test focuses on speculative events and constructing/sending customized messages for speculative events. The requirement specifically concerns evolutionary events; this test does not demonstrate notification behavior for evolutionary events.

Description: RQ132 For speculative events; the eventserver shall construct a composite event message.
Reason: TC150 addresses subscriber manager receiving a speculative event notification. It does not provide evidence that the event server constructs a composite event message.

Description: RQ132 For speculative events; the eventserver shall construct a composite event message.
Reason: TC151 covers user construction of a speculative query and that a speculative event message is received by the event server; it does not verify that the event server constructs a composite event message.

Description: RQ132 For speculative events; the eventserver shall construct a composite event message.
Reason: TC152 exercises subscriber manager processing of a speculative event (parsing, injecting values, re-executing models). It does not assert that the event server constructs a composite event message.

Description: RQ132 For speculative events; the eventserver shall construct a composite event message.
Reason: TC159 verifies receipt, parsing, categorization, and logging of events by the event server, including identifying speculative events, but does not assert construction of a composite event message.

Description: RQ132 For speculative events; the eventserver shall construct a composite event message.
Reason: TC162 explicitly states the event server receives a speculative event and then 'constructs a composite event message' including the changed requirement plus quantitative constraints from related requirements and sends the customized message to subscribers. This directly and completely exercises the requirement behavior.

Description: RQ133 The eventserver shall identify all related requirements and constraints for each impacted executable model.
Reason: This test describes real-time publication of an event to subscribers but does not describe identifying related requirements or their constraints for impacted executable models.

Description: RQ133 The eventserver shall identify all related requirements and constraints for each impacted executable model.
Reason: This test covers decomposition of a requirement and sending a decompose event, but does not show the eventserver identifying related requirements and constraints for impacted executable models.

Description: RQ133 The eventserver shall identify all related requirements and constraints for each impacted executable model.
Reason: This test describes a user-driven refine event and creation of related requirements and temporary links; it does not show the eventserver identifying related requirements/constraints for impacted executable models.

Description: RQ133 The eventserver shall identify all related requirements and constraints for each impacted executable model.
Reason: This test covers merging requirements and reassignment of links; it does not demonstrate the eventserver identifying related requirements and constraints for impacted executable models.

Description: RQ133 The eventserver shall identify all related requirements and constraints for each impacted executable model.
Reason: This test explicitly states the event server identifies impacted requirements and finds subscribers, which maps to the requirement's 'identify ... related requirements' portion. It does not mention identifying constraints, nor does it explicitly mention 'executable model' (uses subscribers instead). Therefore it partially covers the requirement.

Description: RQ133 The eventserver shall identify all related requirements and constraints for each impacted executable model.
Reason: This test contains explicit steps where the event server identifies additional related requirements and obtains quantitative constraints (via DOORS) and includes quantitative constraints in a composite message. That directly addresses identification of related requirements and constraints. It does not explicitly state these steps are performed 'for each impacted executable model' (it uses 'subscriber' and 'artifact' terminology), and it does not assert completeness ('all' related requirements) or error/failure modes, so coverage is partial.

Description: RQ134 The eventserver shall retrieve quantitative values related to these additional requirements and constraints through querying the requirements management tool.
Reason: This test verifies real-time event publication to subscribers but does not state that the event server queries a requirements management tool for quantitative values.

Description: RQ134 The eventserver shall retrieve quantitative values related to these additional requirements and constraints through querying the requirements management tool.
Reason: This test describes adding a new requirement and sending an event message; there is no mention of querying the requirements management tool or retrieving quantitative values.

Description: RQ134 The eventserver shall retrieve quantitative values related to these additional requirements and constraints through querying the requirements management tool.
Reason: This test describes decomposing a requirement and message sending; it does not mention querying a requirements management tool or retrieving quantitative values.

Description: RQ134 The eventserver shall retrieve quantitative values related to these additional requirements and constraints through querying the requirements management tool.
Reason: This test describes a user-driven refine action and creation of related requirements and messages, but contains no statement about the eventserver querying a requirements management tool or retrieving quantitative values.

Description: RQ134 The eventserver shall retrieve quantitative values related to these additional requirements and constraints through querying the requirements management tool.
Reason: The test verifies that the event server identifies impacted requirements and subscribers (related preparatory steps), which is related but does not assert that the event server queries the requirements management tool or retrieves quantitative values/constraints.

Description: RQ134 The eventserver shall retrieve quantitative values related to these additional requirements and constraints through querying the requirements management tool.
Reason: The test explicitly states the event server 'polls DOORS to obtain additional quantitative information' and uses 'quantitative constraints obtained from other related requirements' to build the message. This directly matches the requirement's mandate to retrieve quantitative values via querying the requirements management tool.

Description: RQ135 The eventserver shall create a composite speculative message which includes the speculative event plus all related requirements and constraint values for each impacted model.
Reason: TC149 covers subscriber manager reception of non-speculative event notifications and logging. It does not relate to constructing composite speculative messages nor inclusion of related requirements/constraint values.

Description: RQ135 The eventserver shall create a composite speculative message which includes the speculative event plus all related requirements and constraint values for each impacted model.
Reason: TC150 describes subscriber manager receipt of a speculative event notification and generic processing. It does not verify that the event server created or populated a composite speculative message with related requirements and constraint values for each impacted model.

Description: RQ135 The eventserver shall create a composite speculative message which includes the speculative event plus all related requirements and constraint values for each impacted model.
Reason: TC151 describes a user constructing a speculative query and that a speculative event message is received by the event server. It does not verify the event server's construction of a composite speculative message containing related requirements and constraint values.

Description: RQ135 The eventserver shall create a composite speculative message which includes the speculative event plus all related requirements and constraint values for each impacted model.
Reason: TC152 describes how a subscriber manager processes a speculative event (parses, injects values, re-executes a model). It does not assert that the event server created a composite speculative message that includes all related requirements and constraint values for each impacted model.

Description: RQ135 The eventserver shall create a composite speculative message which includes the speculative event plus all related requirements and constraint values for each impacted model.
Reason: TC159 asserts the event server receives, parses, categorizes (speculative vs evolutionary), and stores events. While it shows reception and categorization, it does not assert creation of a composite speculative message containing all related requirements and constraint values for impacted models.

Description: RQ135 The eventserver shall create a composite speculative message which includes the speculative event plus all related requirements and constraint values for each impacted model.
Reason: TC162 explicitly states the event server constructs a composite event message including the changed requirement and quantitative constraints from related requirements and describes polling DOORS and customizing per subscriber. This directly maps to the requirement's core behaviour. It does not, however, fully assert boundary/error behaviours (e.g., verification that "all" related requirements are included for every impacted model in multi-model scenarios, or error handling when DOORS polling fails), so confidence is partial.

Description: RQ136 The eventserver shall send the customized message to each impacted executable model.
Reason: TC152 demonstrates that a subscriber manager can receive and process a speculative event and return a response (showing the subscriber-side handling). However, it does not demonstrate that the eventserver constructs or sends the customized message to each impacted executable model; reception by subscriber manager is evidence of a message being received but does not prove the eventserver performed the required customization/sending behavior.

Description: RQ136 The eventserver shall send the customized message to each impacted executable model.
Reason: TC159 verifies event reception and categorization by the event server (preconditions for later actions) but contains no assertion that the server constructs a customized message or sends it to impacted executable models.

Description: RQ136 The eventserver shall send the customized message to each impacted executable model.
Reason: TC160 verifies the event server identifies impacted requirements and locates subscribers (the 'each impacted' identification step), but it does not assert construction of a customized message nor explicit sending to executable models. It provides necessary precursor behavior but not the send/customization action required by RQ136.

Description: RQ136 The eventserver shall send the customized message to each impacted executable model.
Reason: TC161 shows the event server sends a notification to each relevant subscriber manager (covering the 'send to each' aspect). However, it specifies a 'notification event message' rather than a 'customized/composite' message to executable models; recipient is a subscriber manager rather than explicitly the executable model, so the match to RQ136 is partial.

Description: RQ136 The eventserver shall send the customized message to each impacted executable model.
Reason: TC162 explicitly describes (a) constructing a composite/customized event message that includes changed requirement plus quantitative constraints and (b) sending that customized event message to the relevant subscriber for each identified subscriber. This directly satisfies both the 'customized message' and 'to each impacted ... model (subscriber)' aspects of RQ136.

Description: RQ137 The user shall view all evolutionary events received for a specific artifact in the order in which they were received.
Reason: TC159 verifies that the event server receives, parses, categorizes (evolutionary vs speculative), and stores events in a log. While it provides evidence that events are recorded and categorized, it does not assert the user-facing display of all evolutionary events in receipt order; therefore it does not by itself satisfy RQ137.

Description: RQ137 The user shall view all evolutionary events received for a specific artifact in the order in which they were received.
Reason: TC161 concerns notifying subscribers when an evolutionary event occurs (notification delivery). It does not assert anything about a user viewing evolutionary events for a specific artifact or about the display ordering, so it does not verify RQ137.

Description: RQ137 The user shall view all evolutionary events received for a specific artifact in the order in which they were received.
Reason: TC162 describes construction of a composite event message for a speculative event. It is about server-side message composition for speculative events and is not related to user viewing of evolutionary events in receipt order.

Description: RQ137 The user shall view all evolutionary events received for a specific artifact in the order in which they were received.
Reason: TC163 explicitly asserts that 'Event messages are displayed in the order in which they were received,' which directly verifies the ordering aspect of RQ137. However, the test does not explicitly state that the displayed messages are limited to 'evolutionary' events (it refers generically to 'event messages'), nor does it explicitly assert that 'all' evolutionary events are shown (precondition only requires 'at least three event messages'). Therefore ordering is verified but scope (evolutionary-only) and completeness ('all') are not fully evidenced.

Description: RQ137 The user shall view all evolutionary events received for a specific artifact in the order in which they were received.
Reason: TC164 checks that the system prohibits processing events out of order. This enforces processing order but does not assert the user view of events or that events are displayed in receipt order; therefore it does not verify RQ137.

Description: RQ138 The user shall update related artifacts according to the information contained in the event messages.
Reason: This test checks that an event is published in real time when a requirement changes. It does not assert that the user updates related artifacts based on the event message content.

Description: RQ138 The user shall update related artifacts according to the information contained in the event messages.
Reason: This test exercises adding a new requirement and sending an event message but contains no assertion that related artifacts are updated by the user in response to event messages.

Description: RQ138 The user shall update related artifacts according to the information contained in the event messages.
Reason: This test asserts that modifying a requirement causes existing links to be changed to tentative status. That demonstrates an update to related artifacts (link status) in response to a modification event. However, it does not assert that the user updates other related artifact contents according to event message information, nor does it verify updates driven by event message content (beyond link status).

Description: RQ138 The user shall update related artifacts according to the information contained in the event messages.
Reason: This test verifies reception, parsing, categorization, and logging of events but contains no assertion that artifacts are updated by the user based on parsed event contents.

Description: RQ138 The user shall update related artifacts according to the information contained in the event messages.
Reason: This test verifies notification delivery to subscriber managers but does not assert that the subscriber or user updates related artifacts according to the event message contents.

Description: RQ138 The user shall update related artifacts according to the information contained in the event messages.
Reason: This test asserts that event messages are enriched with information from related requirements (quantitative constraints from other related requirements). That shows event messages can contain information needed to update related artifacts, but the test does not assert that a user (or system) actually updates related artifacts according to that message. Therefore it partially supports the requirement (message content) but does not demonstrate the required user update action.

Description: RQ138 The user shall update related artifacts according to the information contained in the event messages.
Reason: This test checks display ordering of event messages. It contains no assertion that a user updates related artifacts based on event message content, so it does not verify the requirement.

Description: RQ139 The user shall eliminate unnecessary traces that were temporarily constructed by the eventserver during initial event processing.
Reason: TC154 explicitly documents temporary links (temporary traces) being established during decomposition, which semantically matches the requirement's subject (temporary traces constructed during initial processing). However, TC154 only asserts creation/establishment of temporary traces and does not assert any elimination or user action to remove unnecessary temporary traces; therefore it provides partial (related) coverage but not verification of the elimination behavior.

Description: RQ139 The user shall eliminate unnecessary traces that were temporarily constructed by the eventserver during initial event processing.
Reason: Test TC157 relates to deleting a requirement and setting existing links to tentative; it does not assert elimination of temporary traces created during initial eventserver processing.
