Description: RQ139 The user shall eliminate unnecessary traces that were temporarily constructed by the eventserver during initial event processing.
Reason: TC159 tests event reception, parsing, categorization, and storage in the event log. It does not assert creation or elimination of temporary traces or user-driven removal.

Description: RQ139 The user shall eliminate unnecessary traces that were temporarily constructed by the eventserver during initial event processing.
Reason: TC160 covers identification of subscribers for an event. It does not address temporary traces or their elimination.

Description: RQ139 The user shall eliminate unnecessary traces that were temporarily constructed by the eventserver during initial event processing.
Reason: Test TC162 describes constructing composite event messages for speculative events. It does not assert creation or elimination of temporary traces by the eventserver during initial processing.

Description: RQ139 The user shall eliminate unnecessary traces that were temporarily constructed by the eventserver during initial event processing.
Reason: Test TC164 covers ordering protections for processing events. It does not mention temporary traces or their elimination.

Description: RQ140 The notification processing system shall automatically eliminate any events that are not longer valid as a result of the user eliminating temporary traces.
Reason: TC147 confirms publishing of events in real time and delivery to subscriber manager. It does not verify deletion/elimination of events triggered by user removal of temporary traces.

Description: RQ140 The notification processing system shall automatically eliminate any events that are not longer valid as a result of the user eliminating temporary traces.
Reason: TC149 asserts that subscriber manager receives and stores event notification messages. It does not test removal/elimination of events based on user actions or temporary traces.

Description: RQ140 The notification processing system shall automatically eliminate any events that are not longer valid as a result of the user eliminating temporary traces.
Reason: TC150 covers receipt and processing of speculative event notifications. It does not assert any automatic elimination of events when temporary traces are removed by the user.

Description: RQ140 The notification processing system shall automatically eliminate any events that are not longer valid as a result of the user eliminating temporary traces.
Reason: TC155 addresses refining a requirement and establishing temporary links; it mentions temporary links being created but contains no assertion that removing those temporary links (or user elimination of temporary traces) causes automatic elimination of events.

Description: RQ140 The notification processing system shall automatically eliminate any events that are not longer valid as a result of the user eliminating temporary traces.
Reason: TC157 describes deleting a requirement and sending a delete event message; it sets requirement status to obsolete and marks links tentative. It does not assert that events are automatically eliminated when the user eliminates temporary traces, nor does it verify that stored events are removed as a result of user action.

Description: RQ140 The notification processing system shall automatically eliminate any events that are not longer valid as a result of the user eliminating temporary traces.
Reason: TC163 verifies display order of events. There is no assertion about deleting or automatically eliminating events when a user removes temporary traces.

Description: RQ140 The notification processing system shall automatically eliminate any events that are not longer valid as a result of the user eliminating temporary traces.
Reason: TC164 checks that events must be processed in order and that out-of-order processing is prohibited. It does not assert any automatic elimination of events, nor any behavior tied to user elimination of temporary traces.
